# Jamango! API — Patterns, UI Guide & Recipes

This file contains tutorials, architecture patterns, UI best practices, and recipes for building games with the Jamango API. For the full API reference (function signatures and types), see `api.ts`. For rules and constraints, see `CLAUDE.md` (or equivalent for your AI tool).

## Table of Contents
- [Patterns & Architecture](#patterns--architecture)
- [UI Guide](#ui-guide)
- [Player Input](#player-input)
- [Leaderboards](#leaderboards)
- [Assets](#assets)
- [Common Recipes](#common-recipes)

---

## Patterns & Architecture

### The Init/Tick System Pattern

Every game system should follow this structure — a module with exported functions, registered in your entry points:

```typescript
// src/server/spawnerSystem.ts
import * as J from "jamango";
import { EnemyTrait } from "../shared/traits";

let spawnTimer = 0;
const SPAWN_INTERVAL = 5; // seconds

export function init() {
  // Setup: register listeners, configure initial state
}

export function tick(dt: number, time: number) {
  spawnTimer += dt;
  if (spawnTimer >= SPAWN_INTERVAL) {
    spawnTimer = 0;
    const enemy = J.spawnProp(J.assets.props["Enemy"].id);
    J.setEntityPosition(enemy, [Math.random() * 20, 5, Math.random() * 20]);
    J.setTrait(enemy, EnemyTrait, { health: 100 });
  }
}

export function onPlayerJoin(playerId: number) {
  // Per-player setup
  J.sendChatMessage("A new player joined!", "#00ff00");
}
```

Register in entry points:
```typescript
// src/server.ts
import * as J from "jamango";
import * as SpawnerSystem from "./server/spawnerSystem";
import * as ScoreSystem from "./server/scoreSystem";
import * as ShopSystem from "./server/shopSystem";

J.onGameStart(() => {
  SpawnerSystem.init();
  ScoreSystem.init();
  ShopSystem.init();
});

J.onGameTick((dt, time) => {
  SpawnerSystem.tick(dt, time);
  ScoreSystem.tick(dt, time);
  ShopSystem.tick(dt, time);
});

J.onPlayerJoin((playerId) => {
  SpawnerSystem.onPlayerJoin(playerId);
  ScoreSystem.onPlayerJoin(playerId);
});
```

Not all systems need all functions. A system may only have `init()`, or only `tick()`.

### Client/Server Data Flow

The typical flow for a multiplayer game action:

1. **Client** detects player intent (input, collision, UI click)
2. **Client** sends a command to the server: `net.send(ACTION, data)`
3. **Server** validates the action and applies state changes via traits
4. **Traits auto-sync** to all clients (batched per tick)
5. **Clients** read updated traits and update visuals/UI

```
Player clicks "Buy Sword"
  → Client: net.send(BUY, { item: "sword" })
  → Server: net.listen(BUY, ...) → validates coins → setTrait(player, InventoryTrait, updated)
  → All clients: getTrait(player, InventoryTrait) reflects new inventory
```

### Trait Composition

Traits are the primary way to attach data to entities. Use them instead of module-level Maps or variables — traits auto-sync across the network.

```typescript
// src/shared/traits.ts
import * as J from "jamango";

// Simple identification trait (no data, no editor UI)
export const PlayerTrait = J.defineTrait("Player");

// Data trait for code use (typed but no editor UI)
export const ScoreTrait = J.defineTrait("Score",
  J.schema.any<{ points: number; streak: number }>()
);

// Editor-visible trait (appears in wrench tool with form controls)
export const SpawnPointTrait = J.defineTrait("SpawnPoint",
  J.schema.object({
    team: J.schema.string({
      label: "Team",
      control: "select",
      options: [
        { value: "red", label: "Red Team" },
        { value: "blue", label: "Blue Team" },
      ],
    }),
    radius: J.schema.number({ label: "Spawn Radius", defaultValue: 5, min: 1, max: 20 }),
  }),
  { name: "Spawn Point", description: "Marks a team spawn location", color: "#ff6600", icon: "📍" }
);
```

Remember: for traits to appear in the editor wrench tool, they MUST:
1. Have `J.schema.object({...})` with field definitions
2. Have metadata (name, description, color, icon) as the third argument
3. Be imported in `client.ts` (directly or via a shared import)

### Command Patterns

Commands handle all client↔server communication:

```typescript
// src/shared/commands.ts
import * as J from "jamango";

// Player action
export const BUY_ITEM = J.net.defineCommand<{ itemKey: string }>("buy_item");

// Server → client notification
export const SHOW_NOTIFICATION = J.net.defineCommand<{ message: string; color: string }>("show_notification");

// Bidirectional state sync
export const SYNC_SCORE = J.net.defineCommand<{ playerId: number; points: number }>("sync_score");
```

```typescript
// src/server.ts — listening and responding
J.net.listen(BUY_ITEM, (data, playerId) => {
  const score = J.getTrait(playerId, ScoreTrait);
  if (!score || score.points < 100) {
    J.net.send(SHOW_NOTIFICATION, { message: "Not enough points!", color: "#ff0000" }, playerId);
    return;
  }
  // Apply purchase...
  J.net.send(SHOW_NOTIFICATION, { message: "Item purchased!", color: "#00ff00" }, playerId);
});
```

```typescript
// src/client.ts — sending and receiving
J.net.send(BUY_ITEM, { itemKey: "sword" });

J.net.listen(SHOW_NOTIFICATION, (data) => {
  showNotification(data.message, data.color); // your UI function
});
```

---

## UI Guide

### Architecture Overview

All UI in Jamango is built with vanilla DOM under `J.uiElement`. No frameworks (React, Vue, Svelte).

**Key principles:**
- One UI entry point — `initUI()` called from `client.ts`
- UI modules export `init()` and update functions — they don't append to DOM on import
- Mount DOM elements once (check if they exist first)
- Bind event listeners once (never inside render/update loops)
- Only re-render when values actually change
- All styles injected via keyed `<style>` tags to prevent duplicates

> **For any UI that updates from `onGameTick` (HUDs, leaderboards, kill feeds, ammo counters), follow the full recipe in `node_modules/jamango/HUD-PATTERN.md`.** It is the only way to keep frame rate on Chromebooks. Short version: build the DOM once, store element refs, use `textContent` with last-value caches, drive animations via CSS `@keyframes` toggled by class. Never write `innerHTML` inside `onGameTick`, never recompute `transform: scale(...)` strings in JS per tick, never use `filter: drop-shadow`.

### Single Bootstrap Pattern

```typescript
// src/client.ts
import * as J from "jamango";
import { initUI } from "./client/ui/uiAPI";

J.onPlayerJoin((playerId) => {
  if (J.net.isClient) {
    initUI();
  }
});
```

```typescript
// src/client/ui/uiAPI.ts
import { initHud } from "./uiHud";
import { initShopPanel } from "./uiShop";
import { initWorldLabels } from "./uiWorldLabels";

export function initUI() {
  if (!J.uiElement) return;
  initHud();
  initShopPanel();
  initWorldLabels();
}
```

### Pointer Lock (Desktop Interaction)

Jamango runs with pointer lock enabled by default (cursor hidden, mouse controls camera). When opening interactive UI panels (shops, inventory, settings), you must release pointer lock so the player can click on DOM elements.

```typescript
// Release cursor for interactive panel
export function openPanel() {
  panelElement.style.display = "block";
  J.setPointerLock("unlocked");
}

// Re-engage pointer lock when panel closes
export function closePanel() {
  panelElement.style.display = "none";
  J.setPointerLock("auto");
}

// Check current pointer lock state
const isLocked = J.getCharacterInput(J.getLocalPlayer())?.pointerLocked;

// Listen for pointer lock changes
document.addEventListener("pointerlockchange", () => {
  const locked = J.getCharacterInput(J.getLocalPlayer())?.pointerLocked;
  // Update UI state based on lock status
});
```

**Pattern for modal dismissal** — close when clicking the background overlay:
```typescript
overlay.addEventListener("pointerdown", (ev) => {
  if ((ev.target as HTMLElement).id === "overlay-bg") {
    closePanel();
  }
});
```

### Mobile Controls

Hide Jamango's built-in mobile controls when you need custom ones:

```typescript
J.setUISettings({
  mobileCameraButton: false,
  mobileRightButtons: false,
  mobileInteractButton: false,
  mobileEmoteButton: false,
});
```

Use `pointerdown` events (not `click`) for buttons — they work on both desktop and mobile without delay:

```typescript
myButton.addEventListener("pointerdown", () => {
  performAction();
});
```

Minimum touch target size: 44x44px. Use viewport units (`vw`, `vh`) for responsive sizing.

### World-Space UI (Waypoints, Labels, Nameplates)

To position DOM elements in 3D world space (floating above entities, waypoint markers, etc.), use `J.getScreenPosition()` inside `J.onGameRender`:

```typescript
// src/client/ui/uiWorldLabels.ts
import * as J from "jamango";
import { addStyle } from "./uiCore";
import { ShopTrait } from "../../shared/traits";

const CSS = `
.world-label {
  position: absolute;
  pointer-events: none;
  font-weight: 900;
  font-size: 14px;
  color: #fff;
  text-shadow: 1px 0 0 #000, 0 -1px 0 #000, 0 1px 0 #000, -1px 0 0 #000;
  transform: translate(-50%, -100%);
}`;

const labels: Map<number, HTMLElement> = new Map();
const screenPos: J.Vec2 = [0, 0];
const offset: J.Vec3 = [0, 3, 0]; // 3 blocks above entity

export function initWorldLabels() {
  addStyle("world-labels", CSS);

  J.onGameRender(() => {
    const shops = J.getAllWithTraits([ShopTrait]);

    for (const [entityId, shopData] of shops) {
      const pos = J.getEntityPosition(entityId);
      if (!pos) continue;

      // Offset position above entity
      const labelPos: J.Vec3 = [pos[0] + offset[0], pos[1] + offset[1], pos[2] + offset[2]];

      // Project to screen
      J.getScreenPosition(screenPos, labelPos);

      // Check if on-screen
      if (screenPos[0] === -1 && screenPos[1] === -1) {
        const el = labels.get(entityId);
        if (el) el.style.display = "none";
        continue;
      }

      // Get or create label element
      let el = labels.get(entityId);
      if (!el) {
        el = document.createElement("div");
        el.className = "world-label";
        el.textContent = shopData.name || "Shop";
        J.uiElement?.appendChild(el);
        labels.set(entityId, el);
      }

      el.style.display = "";
      el.style.left = `${Math.round(screenPos[0])}px`;
      el.style.top = `${Math.round(screenPos[1])}px`;
    }
  });
}
```

### Text Readability

Text displayed over a 3D scene needs to be readable regardless of the background. Always use text-shadow:

```css
.game-text {
  color: #fff;
  font-weight: 900;
  text-shadow: 1px 0 0 #000, 0 -1px 0 #000, 0 1px 0 #000, -1px 0 0 #000;
}
```

For panels and HUD elements over the 3D scene, use semi-transparent dark backgrounds:

```css
.hud-panel {
  background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.7);
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 8px 12px;
}
```

### Building a HUD or other always-visible UI

See **`node_modules/jamango/HUD-PATTERN.md`** for the complete recipe (style tag, persistent refs, `textContent` + last-value cache, CSS `@keyframes` triggered by class toggles, anti-patterns to avoid). Every always-visible UI in a Jamango game should follow it. The World-Space UI example above uses the same pattern, applied to entity-tracking labels.

---

## Player Input

### Keyboard Input (Desktop)

Use `J.onControlPress` and `J.onControlRelease` for key bindings — not raw DOM events:

```typescript
// Client-side only
J.onControlPress("q", (playerId) => {
  if (playerId !== J.getLocalPlayer()) return;
  useAbility();
});

J.onControlRelease("q", (playerId) => {
  if (playerId !== J.getLocalPlayer()) return;
  stopAbility();
});
```

### Continuous Input (Movement, Camera)

Use `J.getCharacterInput()` to read the player's current input state:

```typescript
J.onGameTick((dt) => {
  const input = J.getCharacterInput(J.getLocalPlayer());
  if (!input) return;

  // input.nipple = { x, y } — movement joystick/WASD
  // input.camera = { x, y } — camera look direction
  // input.pointerLocked — whether pointer lock is active
});
```

### Cross-Platform Pattern (Desktop + Mobile)

Key bindings (`onControlPress`) don't work on mobile. For custom actions that need to work on both platforms, extract the logic into a shared function:

```typescript
function useAbility() {
  // action logic here
  net.send(USE_ABILITY, {});
}

// Desktop: key binding
J.onControlPress("q", (playerId) => {
  if (playerId !== J.getLocalPlayer()) return;
  useAbility();
});

// Mobile: UI button (inside J.uiElement)
const abilityBtn = document.createElement("button");
abilityBtn.textContent = "⚡";
abilityBtn.style.cssText = "position:absolute;bottom:80px;right:16px;width:56px;height:56px;font-size:24px;border-radius:50%;border:3px solid #000;background:#FFD700;pointer-events:auto;";
abilityBtn.addEventListener("pointerdown", () => useAbility());
J.uiElement?.appendChild(abilityBtn);
```

---

## Leaderboards

Leaderboards persist scores across sessions. Define in shared code, set scores on the server, read on client.

### Full Flow Example

```typescript
// src/shared/leaderboards.ts
import * as J from "jamango";

export const HighScoreBoard = J.leaderboards.defineLeaderboard("high_scores", {
  mode: "highest", // or "lowest" for time trials
});
```

```typescript
// src/server.ts — set scores (server only, integer scores only)
import { HighScoreBoard } from "./shared/leaderboards";

J.onGameTick((dt, time) => {
  // When a player completes a round...
  const finalScore = Math.round(playerPoints);
  J.leaderboards.setPlayerScore(HighScoreBoard, playerId, finalScore);
});
```

```typescript
// src/client.ts — read and display scores (async API)
import { HighScoreBoard } from "./shared/leaderboards";

async function showLeaderboard() {
  // Top scores globally
  const topScores = await J.leaderboards.getTopScores(HighScoreBoard);
  topScores.forEach((entry) => {
    console.log(`#${entry.place}: ${entry.playerUsername} — ${entry.score}`);
  });

  // Current player's score
  const myScore = await J.leaderboards.getPlayerScore(HighScoreBoard, J.getLocalPlayer());
  if (myScore) {
    console.log(`Your rank: #${myScore.place}, Score: ${myScore.score}`);
  }

  // Scores for all currently connected players
  const playerScores = await J.leaderboards.getScoresForPlayers(HighScoreBoard);
}
```

**Key notes:**
- `setPlayerScore` is server-only and only accepts integers (`Math.round` is applied internally)
- Scores only update if better than previous (higher for "highest" mode, lower for "lowest")
- The API is async — `getTopScores`, `getPlayerScore`, `getScoresForPlayers` return Promises
- Leaderboard definitions must be in shared code so both client and server have access

---

## Assets

### Referencing World Assets

Every block, prop, audio clip, avatar, particle system, environment preset, and emote in your world has an asset ID. Access them via `J.assets`:

```typescript
// Props
const coinId = J.assets.props["Coin"].id;
const coin = J.spawnProp(coinId);

// Blocks
const grassId = J.assets.blocks["Grass"].id;
J.setBlock([5, 0, 5], J.BLOCK_SHAPE_CUBE, grassId);

// Audio
J.playSound(J.assets.audio["BackgroundMusic"].id, { volume: 0.3, loop: true });

// Environment presets
J.setEnvironmentPreset(J.assets.environmentPresets["NightTime"].id);

// Emotes
J.characterPlayEmote(playerId, J.assets.emotes["Wave"].id);

// Avatars
const npc = J.spawnCharacter(J.assets.avatars["Guard"].id);

// Avatar components (hats, torsos, etc.)
const hatId = J.assets.avatarComponents["CoolHat"].id;
```

### How Asset IDs Work

Asset IDs are strings like `"prop#6CA2D1B994904AFBB3699EB2AA67D62B"`. You don't need to memorize them — use the name-based lookup via `J.assets`. The `assets.ts` file in `node_modules/jamango/` contains all asset IDs for your current world and refreshes every 10 seconds when connected to the editor.

### Prop Icons for UI

To display a prop's icon in your UI (e.g., an inventory system):

```typescript
const iconUrl = J.getPropAssetIcon(J.assets.props["Sword"].id);
if (iconUrl) {
  const img = document.createElement("img");
  img.src = iconUrl; // base64-encoded image
  inventorySlot.appendChild(img);
}
```

---

## Common Recipes

### Collection Mechanic

Spawn collectible items and track pickup count per player:

```typescript
import * as J from "jamango";

const PlayerTrait = J.defineTrait("Player", J.schema.object({
  coins: J.schema.number(),
}));
const CoinTrait = J.defineTrait("Coin");

J.onPlayerJoin((playerId) => {
  J.setTrait(playerId, PlayerTrait, { coins: 0 });
});

J.onGameStart(() => {
  for (let i = 0; i < 20; i++) {
    const coin = J.spawnProp(J.assets.props["Coin"].id);
    J.setEntityPosition(coin, [Math.random() * 30, 1, Math.random() * 30]);
    J.setEntityScale(coin, 0.3);
    J.setTrait(coin, CoinTrait);
  }
});

J.onEntityCollisionStart(
  { source: [CoinTrait], target: [PlayerTrait] },
  (coinId, playerId) => {
    J.removeEntity(coinId);
    const player = J.getTrait(playerId, PlayerTrait);
    player.coins += 1;
    J.setTrait(playerId, PlayerTrait, player);
    J.sendChatMessage(`Collected! Total: ${player.coins}`, "#FFD700", [playerId]);
  }
);
```

### Obby Checkpoints and Hazards

Store player progression and respawn on hazard collision:

```typescript
import * as J from "jamango";

const PlayerCheckpointTrait = J.defineTrait("playerCheckpoint", J.schema.object({
  spawn: J.schema.vec3(),
  checkpoint: J.schema.number(),
}));
const CheckpointTrait = J.defineTrait("checkpoint", J.schema.number());
const HazardTrait = J.defineTrait("hazard");

J.onPlayerJoin((playerId) => {
  J.setTrait(playerId, PlayerCheckpointTrait, {
    spawn: [0, 10, 0],
    checkpoint: -1,
  });
});

// Checkpoint reached — update spawn
J.onEntityCollisionStart(
  { source: [PlayerCheckpointTrait], target: [CheckpointTrait] },
  (playerId, checkpointId) => {
    const player = J.getTrait(playerId, PlayerCheckpointTrait);
    const checkpointLevel = J.getTrait(checkpointId, CheckpointTrait);
    if (player.checkpoint < checkpointLevel) {
      player.spawn = J.getEntityPosition(checkpointId);
      player.checkpoint = checkpointLevel;
      J.setTrait(playerId, PlayerCheckpointTrait, player);
    }
  }
);

// Hazard hit — respawn at last checkpoint
J.onEntityCollisionStart(
  { source: [PlayerCheckpointTrait], target: [HazardTrait] },
  (playerId) => {
    const player = J.getTrait(playerId, PlayerCheckpointTrait);
    J.setEntityPosition(playerId, player.spawn);
  }
);
```

### Timed Spawning (Without setInterval)

Use `J.onGameTick` with time tracking:

```typescript
const SPAWN_INTERVAL = 2; // seconds
let lastSpawnTime = 0;

J.onGameTick((dt, time) => {
  if (time - lastSpawnTime >= SPAWN_INTERVAL) {
    lastSpawnTime = time;
    const prop = J.spawnProp(J.assets.props["Enemy"].id);
    J.setEntityPosition(prop, [Math.random() * 20, 5, Math.random() * 20]);
  }
});
```

### Bouncy Trait (Entity Collision)

Make players bounce off entities tagged with a trait:

```typescript
const BouncyTrait = J.defineTrait("Bouncy",
  J.schema.object({ force: J.schema.number({ defaultValue: 10, label: "Force" }) }),
  { name: "Bouncy", description: "Bounces entities upward", color: "#ffe100", icon: "🦘" }
);
const PlayerTrait = J.defineTrait("Player");

J.onPlayerJoin((playerId) => {
  J.setTrait(playerId, PlayerTrait);
});

J.onEntityCollisionStart(
  { source: [BouncyTrait], target: [PlayerTrait] },
  (bouncyId, playerId) => {
    const bouncy = J.getTrait(bouncyId, BouncyTrait);
    J.addEntityVelocity(playerId, [0, bouncy.force, 0]);
  }
);
```

### Bouncy Block Type

Apply bounce to a specific block type:

```typescript
const BouncyBlockTrait = J.defineTrait("BouncyBlock");
const PlayerTrait = J.defineTrait("Player");

J.onPlayerJoin((playerId) => {
  J.setTrait(playerId, PlayerTrait);
});

J.onGameStart(() => {
  J.setTrait(J.assets.blocks["Trampoline"].id, BouncyBlockTrait);
});

J.onBlockCollisionStart(
  { source: [BouncyBlockTrait], target: [PlayerTrait] },
  (_position, _normal, _blockType, entityId) => {
    J.addEntityVelocity(entityId, [0, 15, 0]);
  }
);
```

### Persistent Player Data

Save and load player progress across sessions:

```typescript
J.onPlayerJoin((playerId, savedData) => {
  if (savedData === undefined) {
    // First time player
    savedData = { coins: 100, level: 1 };
    J.setPlayerPersistentData(playerId, savedData);
  }
  J.sendChatMessage(`Welcome back! You have ${savedData.coins} coins.`, "#00FF00", [playerId]);
});

// Save periodically
const SAVE_INTERVAL = 10;
let lastSave = 0;

J.onGameTick((dt, time) => {
  if (time - lastSave < SAVE_INTERVAL) return;
  lastSave = time;

  for (const playerId of J.getAllPlayers()) {
    const score = J.getTrait(playerId, ScoreTrait);
    if (score) {
      J.setPlayerPersistentData(playerId, { coins: score.points, level: score.level });
    }
  }
});
```

Note: Play mode and edit mode have **separate** persistent data storage.

### Swimming (Global Water Level)

Apply upward force when a player is below a certain Y level:

```typescript
const PlayerTrait = J.defineTrait("Player");
const WATER_LEVEL = -1;

J.onPlayerJoin((playerId) => {
  J.setTrait(playerId, PlayerTrait);
});

J.onGameTick(() => {
  for (const [playerId] of J.getAllWithTraits([PlayerTrait])) {
    const pos = J.getEntityPosition(playerId);
    if (pos && pos[1] < WATER_LEVEL) {
      J.addEntityVelocity(playerId, [0, 1, 0]);
    }
  }
});
```

### Out-of-Bounds Respawn

Reset players who fall out of the world:

```typescript
J.onGameTick(() => {
  const spawn = J.getWorldSpawn();
  for (const id of J.getAllCharacters()) {
    if (!J.isInWorldBounds(J.getEntityPosition(id))) {
      J.setEntityPosition(id, spawn.position, true);
    }
  }
});
```
